skill.name: twitter-automation
skill.description: Automate Twitter/X with posting, engagement, and user management via inference.sh CLI. Apps: x/post-tweet, x/post-create (with media), x/post-like, x/post-retweet, x/dm-send, x/user-follow. Capabilities: post tweets, schedule content, like posts, retweet, send DMs, follow users, get profiles.
skill.summary: Automate Twitter/X posting, engagement, and user management through Claude Code
skill.author: toolshell

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 45 potential issues (37 external_commands, 6 network, 2 weak crypto). All findings are false positives from markdown code blocks and documentation URLs. The skill contains only documentation examples for the inference.sh CLI tool with no executable code. No security risks confirmed.

content.value_statement: Manually posting to Twitter and managing engagement is time-consuming and error-prone. This skill automates tweet posting, content scheduling, likes, retweets, DMs, and user management through Claude Code using the inference.sh CLI.
actual_capabilities: Post text tweets and media to Twitter/X
limitations: Requires inference.sh CLI installation and authentication
Requires valid Twitter/X API credentials configured in inference.sh
Rate limits apply based on Twitter/X API tier
Cannot bypass Twitter/X content moderation or policy restrictions

use_cases.title: Social Media Manager Automation
use_cases.description: Schedule and post content across multiple accounts, automate engagement by liking and retweeting relevant posts, and manage follower growth without manual intervention.
use_cases.target_user: Social media managers and marketing teams

use_cases.title: Developer Content Publishing
use_cases.description: Automatically post project updates, announce releases, and share code snippets directly from your development workflow through Claude Code.

use_cases.title: AI-Generated Content Workflow
use_cases.description: Generate AI images or videos using inference.sh apps, then automatically post them to Twitter with descriptions in a seamless pipeline.
use_cases.target_user: AI content creators and automation enthusiasts

prompt_templates.title: Post a Simple Tweet
prompt_templates.prompt: Post this tweet: "Just shipped a new feature! Check it out"

prompt_templates.title: Post with Media
prompt_templates.prompt: Post this to Twitter with the image at https://example.com/image.jpg: "Look at this AI-generated artwork!"

prompt_templates.title: Engagement Automation
prompt_templates.prompt: Like and retweet the tweet with ID 1234567890

prompt_templates.title: User Management
prompt_templates.prompt: Send a DM to user @johndoe saying "Thanks for the follow!" and then follow their account

output_examples.input: Post a tweet saying "Hello from Claude Code!"

best_practices: Test Twitter automation with a test account first to avoid accidental posts from your main account
Respect Twitter rate limits by spacing out automated posts and engagement actions
Use descriptive text when posting media to improve accessibility and engagement
anti_patterns: Do not post duplicate content in rapid succession as this may trigger Twitter spam filters
Avoid automating follows or unfollows in bulk as this violates Twitter terms of service
Never include sensitive credentials or API keys directly in your prompt commands

faq.question: Do I need a Twitter developer account?
faq.answer: Yes, you need a Twitter/X developer account with API access. Configure your credentials in the inference.sh platform before using this skill.

faq.question: Can I schedule tweets for future posting?
faq.answer: This skill posts immediately. For scheduling, use Claude Code to create the content, then use a scheduling service or combine with a cron job to trigger posts at specific times.

faq.question: What Twitter rate limits apply?
faq.answer: Rate limits depend on your Twitter API tier. Basic tier allows 50 posts per day. Check your API tier limits to avoid being throttled.

faq.question: Can I post images and videos?
faq.answer: Yes, use x/post-create with a media_url parameter. The skill supports posting images, videos, and other media hosted at accessible URLs.

faq.question: Is my Twitter data secure?
faq.answer: Your credentials are stored securely in the inference.sh platform, not in this skill. The skill only triggers the CLI tool with your authorization.

faq.question: Can I manage multiple Twitter accounts?
faq.answer: Yes, if you configure multiple Twitter accounts in inference.sh. Specify which account to use when setting up your authentication.
